assertions.description: The analyzer correctly reports: board 19.05mm x 7.62mm (standard SparkFun Micro form factor), 2 copper layers (F.Cu, B.Cu), 35 footprints, 92 track segments, 15 vias, 1 zone, 17 nets, routing_complete=true, 0 unrouted nets. SMD count 17 and THT count 2 (J1 and J5 are PTH connectors, standoff ST1 is another) — actually smd_count=17 and tht_count=2 seems low given ST1 is a standoff hole but board_only items may be excluded.

The PCB net list correctly contains 17 nets matching the schematic: 3.3V, GND, SDA, SCL, ADDR/SDO, INT1, Net-(D1-A), Net-(JP1-A), Net-(JP2-A), Net-(JP2-C), and 5 unconnected-* nets for U1 unused pins plus 2 unconnected Qwiic NC pins. The unconnected nets for ADC1/ADC2/ADC3/CS/INT2 on U1 and the NC pins on J2 correctly match the schematic no-connects.

INCORRECT: The schematic has 11 real SMD components (U1, C3, C4, D1, R1, R2, R3, R6, JP1, JP2, JP3, J2, FID3, FID4) plus logo footprints. The PCB reports smd_count=17 and tht_count=2. The tht_count=2 appears to count only J1 and J5 (the two PTH pin connectors), while ST1 (standoff) may be excluded or treated differently. This count is likely correct for the actual through-hole pads present, but is worth noting as the standoff footprint type may vary.
